title: erigeron bilbaoanus
intro: Erigeron bilbaoanus, also known as the Bilbao daisy or poverty weed, is a flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the western coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Erigeron bilbaoanus include its potential to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. Erigeron bilbaoanus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
